summary: Backend-only regression for GridFS uploads, file serving, unsubscribe, founder settings, and newsletter HTML. 24/24 pytest cases pass. All review items verified: image/PDF roundtrip via GridFS, Content-Type headers correct, non-existent file returns 404, unsubscribe flips newsletter flag and rejects bad input with 400, /api/unsubscribe present in newsletter HTML, height:100px present in newsletter, founder block toggles correctly with enabled flag and contains 'A note from the founder' + intro + name, all admin endpoints reject unauthenticated requests with 401, regression endpoints (login, /api/articles, /api/subscribers, /api/auth/me) all OK.

minor.issue: BACKEND_URL env var is not set in backend/.env, so the returned image_url / pdf_url are relative paths like '/api/files/<uuid>.png'. This works in-app (frontend ingress routes /api to backend) but BREAKS in email clients (newsletter founder photo, article images embedded in emails) because mail clients cannot resolve a relative URL. Recommend setting BACKEND_URL=FRONTEND_URL in backend/.env so absolute URLs are returned, OR prefixing FRONTEND_URL when rendering URLs inside email HTML.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Set BACKEND_URL in backend/.env (e.g., same value as FRONTEND_URL) so /api/upload/image and /api/upload/pdf return absolute URLs. Without it, founder photos uploaded via /api/upload/image will render as broken images in dispatched newsletters because relative '/api/files/...' URLs do not resolve inside email clients.

OPTIONAL code-quality: CORS middleware is configured with both an explicit origin AND '*' in allow_origins while allow_credentials=True (server.py ~2284). Browsers will reject this combination for credentialed requests; the explicit FRONTEND_URL alone is sufficient.
OPTIONAL: server.py is 2393 lines; consider splitting into modules (auth, articles, files, newsletter, subscribers, rss, settings) for maintainability.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1059 upload_image and 1031 upload_pdf use os.environ.get('BACKEND_URL', '') -- this env var is missing, returning relative URLs that won't render in email.
server.py:2284 CORS allow_origins mixes an explicit origin with '*' while allow_credentials=True; browser will reject. Remove '*'.
server.py:1264 subscribe welcome email correctly uses {base_url}/api/unsubscribe (good).
server.py:1097-1099 serve_file falls back to local disk for legacy files only -- acceptable; new uploads go to GridFS as required.
seed_data_creation: Created and then cleaned up subscribers with emails 'test_unsub_<rand>@example.com' and 'test_sub_render_<rand>@example.com'. Founder settings were left with enabled=true, name='Jane Founder', intro_text='A weekly word from the founder about Iran.', title='Director' from the last test -- main agent may want to reset these via PUT /api/settings/founder if shipping to production.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (24 tests). Uses requests Session; uses a fresh_client fixture for unauth checks because the shared `api` session retains login cookies after /api/auth/login. Frontend external URL: https://iran-events-live.preview.emergentagent.com. The minimal in-test PNG and PDF builders avoid Pillow/reportlab deps. The cleanup fixture removes test_* subscribers at session end. Founder settings persist in Mongo and were last set with name='Jane Founder' (see seed_data_creation note).
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs reproduced. All requested features verified working end-to-end.
